---
title: "anger management reset"
format: html
---

## Anger Management Reset

When anger is high, decision quality drops. A reset routine creates space between feeling and action.

This is not suppression. It is a short sequence to reduce intensity so you can choose a response aligned with your values.

Interpersonal stress is a common relapse risk. Communication habits, emotional reactivity, and body language all affect how quickly conflict escalates and how supported you feel afterward.

When communication becomes clearer and more regulated, stress drops and problem-solving improves. These skills protect recovery by reducing isolation, resentment, and impulsive reactions during difficult interactions.

::: callout
### Instructions
Practice this sequence when anger reaches 6/10 or higher.
:::

1. Step away from the trigger for 10 minutes.
2. Slow breathing: inhale 4 seconds, exhale 6 seconds for 2 minutes.
3. Name the core need (respect, safety, fairness, being heard).
4. Choose one assertive sentence instead of reacting.
5. Re-engage only when intensity drops below 4/10.

### Practice Log

| Situation | Anger before | Anger after reset | What helped most |
|---|---|---|---|
| | | | |
| | | | |
| | | | |
